title: Workflow, job, and step execution model
description: Jobs run on separate runners in parallel by default; steps share a runner and filesystem; needs creates ordering and artifact handoff.
body: Use when structuring or debugging a multi-job workflow. A workflow contains jobs; jobs run on independent runners (fresh filesystem, parallel unless ordered by needs); steps within a job share that runner's filesystem and environment. State does not cross jobs except via artifacts, outputs, or external storage. Do not assume a file written in one job exists in another, and do not rely on step order across jobs without needs. Actions: use job outputs for small values, upload/download-artifact for files, and needs to express dependency and gating. Validation signals include a downstream job receiving the expected output/artifact and parallel jobs not racing on shared external state. Risk boundary: cross-job data passed through a mutable shared store can be tampered by an untrusted parallel job; treat it as untrusted input.
